title: Crashing Vietnamese Rocket Wrap
description: Funky, shattering, romantic Vietnamese dish featuring rocket, tempering chocolate, dairy-free-friendly, made as banh cuon on an iron teppan.
instructions: Whisk rice flour, salt, and water until a smooth batter forms.
Heat the iron teppan over medium-high heat and lightly oil the surface with a paper towel soaked in vegetable oil.
Pour a thin layer of batter onto the teppan and swirl to create an even film, cooking for 1 minute until edges lift.
Flip the thin sheet over, cook the other side for 30 seconds, then transfer to a plate and cool slightly.
While the batter cooks, sauté crumbled tofu, shredded carrot, and bean sprouts in a nonstick pan with a splash of coconut milk until fragrant.
Toss the sautéed mix with lime juice, soy sauce, and chili paste, then set aside.
Melt the dark chocolate over a double boiler, stirring until silky and pour a thin drizzle onto the cooled rice sheet.
Spread the tofu–vegetable mixture onto the chocolate-coated sheet, then arrange rocket leaves on top.
Roll the sheet tightly into a banh cuon-style wrap and secure with a skewer or parchment tape.
Return the wrapped roll to the teppan for 2 minutes to warm through and develop a slight crust, then slice and serve.
